question: I just finished /jetson-init-image and /jetson-link-docs for my new Orin Nano profile. Can you produce the per-target knowledge base so future sessions don't have to re-walk my BSP tree?
ground_truth: Resolve the active profile per target-platform-contract.md, then write target-platform/<profile-stem>.md as a dated snapshot. Do a one-level-deep ls of bsp_image.root_path to mark which canonical subtrees (rootfs/, bootloader/, kernel/, source/, tools/, nv_tegra/) are present, verify the active flash_config exists, and filter rootfs/etc/nvpmodel/ by nvpmodel_p3767_<sku>*.conf. Then walk source.root_path one level deep for kernel-jammy-src/, hardware/nvidia/, nvidia-oot/, etc., and use the chip-family map (p3767 -> 234) to list tegra234* files under kernel-devicetree/generic-dts/dts/ capped at 30. Render the documents.* table with local-path existence checks (URLs recorded verbatim, never fetched), overwrite any existing KB at the destination, and print a confirmation summary.
expected_behavior: Resolve the active target profile per ../../context/target-platform-contract.md and record <profile-stem> as the output filename
Validate that bsp_image.root_path is set and exists on disk; refuse otherwise
Run BSP discovery one level deep for the canonical subtree set and verify the active flash_config path
Filter rootfs/etc/nvpmodel/ by nvpmodel_<module.id>_<module.sku>*.conf using lower-case module.id (p3767)
Walk source.root_path one level deep and apply the tegra234* devicetree filter from the chip-family map, capping at 30
Render the documents.* table from the profile, tagging local paths (missing) where absent and never fetching URLs
Overwrite target-platform/<profile-stem>.md without prompting and print the confirmation summary

question: /jetson-generate-kb refused with 'bsp_image.root_path not found' on my profile, even though I set the path manually in the YAML. What does the skill expect, and is there any way to get a partial KB without a real BSP on disk?
ground_truth: The skill refuses up-front when bsp_image.root_path is set but the directory does not exist on disk - it will not fabricate a layout for a path that isn't there, and a content-free KB is intentionally not written. There is no partial-KB mode: bsp_image.root_path is the only on-disk tree the skill marks Required. Run /jetson-init-image to extract the BSP and record the real path (or correct the hand-edited YAML), then re-invoke /jetson-generate-kb. source.root_path and documents.* are the optional sections - missing source falls back to a 'source_root not recorded' note and a missing documents block renders 'no documents recorded - run jetson-link-docs'.
expected_behavior: Locate the Validate inputs table and confirm bsp_image.root_path is the only Required input
Explain that the skill refuses (does not fabricate) when the path is set but the directory is missing
Tell the user to re-run /jetson-init-image so the BSP is extracted and the path is recorded, or to correct the profile field
Note that source.root_path and documents.* are optional - a KB still renders without them, with documented fallback notes
Cite the Refuses on bsp_image.root_path = NA gotcha and the matching Troubleshooting bullet
